name: 3Dmol.js
when_to_use: 需要在浏览器中旋转、缩放并以 cartoon 或球棍方式查看 PDB/SDF/MOL2/XYZ 三维坐标时用它而非手写 WebGL,因为原生在分子解析、键连接、相机与着色上失败;非做 2D 结构编辑或化学性质计算。
why_valuable: AI 默认只能复述坐标、原子或残基列表,无法传达分子折叠与空间邻近关系;3Dmol.js 把多格式解析、分子样式、相机控制和 WebGL 场景闭环。

en.when_to_use: Use it when you need to rotate and zoom PDB/SDF/MOL2/XYZ 3D coordinates in the browser and view them as cartoons or ball-and-stick models instead of hand-writing WebGL, which fails at molecule parsing, bond connectivity, cameras, and shading; not for 2D structure editing or chemical-property calculations.
en.why_valuable: AI can normally only restate coordinates or lists of atoms or residues, not convey molecular folding and spatial proximity; 3Dmol.js closes the loop with multi-format parsing, molecular styles, camera controls, and a WebGL scene.

data_in.note: source must be the file contents, not a URL; format is passed directly to GLViewer.addModel so the component does not have to guess the format.

schema.source: string -- complete molecular coordinate/structure file contents

spec.note: The WebGL canvas requires a container with a pixel height; default height is 420px, and width is determined by the container. Do not use vh or percentage heights. Call viewer.resize() after the container size changes.

data_in.note: source 必须是文件正文而非 URL;format 直接传给 GLViewer.addModel,避免让组件猜格式。

schema.source: string —— 完整分子坐标/结构文件正文

spec.note: WebGL canvas 依赖有像素高度的容器;默认 height 420px,width 由容器决定,禁用 vh/百分比高。容器尺寸变化后调用 viewer.resize()。

boot.codex: 同 artifact(sandbox iframe):ESM 导入 3Dmol,显式 420px 容器,createViewer → addModel(source,format) → setStyle → zoomTo/render。

carriers.widget: conditional:需宿主 WebGL;canvas 落 shadow 容器,无 CSS/font/uplink 足迹
carriers.codex: conditional:需 sandbox WebGL;数据内联时无额外网络请求

isolation.evidence: +esm 实测:`document.body.addEventListener('mouseup',this._handleMouse...)` 与 `window.addEventListener('resize',this.resize.bind(this))`;grep 无 createElement('style')/document.head,WebGL canvas 渲入传入容器(可为 shadow)。故渲染 shadow-OK 但残留全局 body/window 监听,且 bind 使 removeEventListener 困难。
